PrincipleName: Policy Governs Standards Implement Procedures Execute
Statement: The Bank shall maintain clear separation between policy authority, standards implementation, and procedure execution.
Rationale: Prevents ambiguity in governance ownership and audit evidence.
RequiredPractice: Map every standard to policy and every procedure to a standard

Statement: Material technology changes shall receive ARB review before implementation.
Rationale: Reduces ungoverned architecture risk and late-stage rework.
RequiredPractice: Require ADR and evidence pack before implementation

Statement: Authoritative data concepts shall have one governed meaning across business capabilities, applications, reporting, and integration.
DecisionUse: Map data objects to authoritative source and data owner

Statement: APIs shall be governed as reusable products with owners, lifecycle states, contracts, security controls, and discoverability.
Rationale: Improves reuse and reduces point-to-point integration risk.
RequiredPractice: Publish API contract and owner in approved catalog

Statement: Security requirements shall be embedded in architecture and engineering design before build, purchase, or implementation.
Rationale: Prevents expensive downstream remediation and reduces cyber/control exposure.
RequiredPractice: Complete security review before design approval

Statement: Technology solutions shall be designed to meet business availability, recovery, observability, and continuity requirements.
Rationale: Supports operational resilience and banking service continuity.
RequiredPractice: Define RTO/RPO/availability and evidence testing before go-live

Statement: Cloud adoption shall use approved landing zones, identity, encryption, network, observability, and cost-management patterns.
Rationale: Avoids unmanaged cloud proliferation and weak control inheritance.
RequiredPractice: Use approved cloud reference architecture and tagging

PrincipleName: Buy Before Build But Do Not Buy Blind
Statement: External product adoption shall consider strategic fit, risk, integration, data, resilience, lifecycle, and exit complexity before approval.
Rationale: Prevents uncontrolled SaaS/vendor sprawl and concentration risk.
RequiredPractice: Perform architecture and third-party review before selection

Statement: Solutions shall provide telemetry, logs, traces, metrics, alerting, and supportability aligned to production criticality.
DecisionUse: Define telemetry and alerting before production release

Statement: Technology lifecycle state shall be visible, governed, and tied to risk acceptance or remediation.
Rationale: EOL/EOS and tolerated technologies create latent operational and security risk.
RequiredPractice: Assign lifecycle owner and remediation plan

Statement: Architecture decisions shall map to business capabilities, value streams, and services before solution selection.
Rationale: Improves investment alignment and reduces local optimization.
RequiredPractice: Map initiative to capability and service model

PrincipleName: Evidence Or It Did Not Happen
Statement: Architecture decisions, controls, exceptions, and approvals shall be supported by retained evidence in approved systems of record.
Owner: Attach evidence to ARB and control objects
